Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
Laboratory results are largely within normal limits. Notable findings include mild hypoglycemia, slightly elevated chloride, and increased LDL cholesterol. Renal function, electrolytes, and lipid profile are otherwise unremarkable. Microalbumin/creatinine ratio is normal, indicating no significant renal impairment. Recommend monitoring glucose and lipid levels, and clinical correlation for hypoglycemia and cardiovascular risk assessment.
